page.title: Template:Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font

revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how-to}}
{{navbar|Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|plain=y}}
 To consistently use a [[monospaced font]] with well-designed characters for coding so as to clearly distinguish between l, 1, and I, and between O and 0, and between -, −, –, and —, the system-default monospaced font can be changed: If like it as-is, you can simply @import (transclude) it: see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/codefont.css]] for quick instructions. Otherwise, copy one of the code snippets below to your [[Special:MyPage/common.css]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.css]] page, replacing {{kbd|"Roboto Mono"}} in the code with your preferred coding font. ([https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Roboto+Mono Roboto Mono] was used in this example as it is good, free coding font, for user-editable material on the wiki). This code will: * Apply a consistent monospace font of choice to all the normally monospaced HTML elements like {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|pre|o}}, etc. * Fallback to system-default {{samp|monospace}} font, should the chosen font be unavailable or lack the necessary characters. * Do the same for the output of all [[:Category:Example-formatting templates|Example-formatting templates]], such as {{tlx|mxt}} and {{tlx|xt}}. * Apply to additional site-wide classes identified so far (e.g., .monospaced) that output as monospace. * Make the three most frequently encountered editing fields also use this font stack: the main editing window, the edit summary line, and the search entry box. * If any additional [[Class (computer programming)|classes]] are known that should be added, please update this page or mention it on [[Template talk:Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|the talk page]]. '''Horizontal style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button, .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} '''Vertical style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} {{anchor|Cleanup efforts}}'''Cleanup efforts''' If you'd like to help clean up instances of the {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}}} element – which has been discouraged since the 1990s, and should usually be replaced with {{tag|{{mxt|code}}}} (this may vary by context) – you can add something like the following to your {{samp|common.css}} to make {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}|o}} stick out like a sore thumb: {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Flag bad code for cleanup */ tt { color: DarkRed; background: Pink; } }} You can also do this with {{tag|font|o}}, {{tag|center|o}}, {{tag|strike|o}}, and other [[Wikipedia:HTML 5#Obsolete elements and attributes|deprecated elements]]. For CSS you can just import for this, see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/lint.css]].{{Documentation|content=This is a documentation snippet page transcluded (without the banner or this doc section) into other template documentation, and into [[Help:User style]], for consistency. It takes no parameters. Typical usage: == User CSS for a monospaced coding font == {{collapse top|left=y|title=Have monospaced templates in this group – and your editing window – use your preferred monospaced font:}} {{Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font}} {{collapse bottom}} It can also be used as a stand-alone how-to page; a redirect to it, [[Help:User CSS for a monospaced coding font]], is categorized as such. It is also transcluded as a section, at [[Help:User style#User CSS for a monospaced coding font]]. {{Wikipedia technical help}} [[Category:Documentation templates]] }}

{{Wikipedia how-to}}
{{navbar|Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|plain=y}}
 To consistently use a [[monospaced font]] with well-designed characters for coding so as to clearly distinguish between l, 1, and I, and between O and 0, and between -, −, –, and —, the system-default monospaced font can be changed: If like it as-is, you can simply @import (transclude) it: see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/codefont.css]] for quick instructions. Otherwise, copy one of the code snippets below to your [[Special:MyPage/common.css]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.css]] page, replacing {{kbd|"Roboto Mono"}} in the code with your preferred coding font. ([https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Roboto+Mono Roboto Mono] was used in this example as it is good, free coding font, for user-editable material on the wiki). This code will: * Apply a consistent monospace font of choice to all the normally monospaced HTML elements like {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|pre|o}}, etc. * Fallback to system-default {{samp|monospace}} font, should the chosen font be unavailable or lack the necessary characters. * Do the same for the output of all [[:Category:Example-formatting templates|Example-formatting templates]], such as {{tlx|mxt}} and {{tlx|xt}}. * Apply to additional site-wide classes identified so far (e.g., .monospaced) that output as monospace. * Make the three most frequently encountered editing fields also use this font stack: the main editing window, the edit summary line, and the search entry box. * If any additional [[Class (computer programming)|classes]] are known that should be added, please update this page or mention it on [[Template talk:Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|the talk page]]. '''Horizontal style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button, .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} '''Vertical style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} {{anchor|Cleanup efforts}}'''Cleanup efforts''' If you'd like to help clean up instances of the {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}}} element – which has been discouraged since the 1990s, and should usually be replaced with {{tag|{{mxt|code}}}} (this may vary by context) – you can add something like the following to your {{samp|common.css}} to make {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}|o}} stick out like a sore thumb: {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Flag bad code for cleanup */ tt { color: DarkRed; background: Pink; } }} You can also do this with {{tag|font|o}}, {{tag|center|o}}, {{tag|strike|o}}, and other [[Wikipedia:HTML 5#Obsolete elements and attributes|deprecated elements]]. For CSS you can just import for this, see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/lint.css]].{{Documentation|content=This is a documentation snippet page transcluded (without the banner or this doc section) into other template documentation, and into [[Help:User style]], for consistency. It takes no parameters. Typical usage: == User CSS for a monospaced coding font == {{collapse top|left=y|title=Have monospaced templates in this group – and your editing window – use your preferred monospaced font:}} {{Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font}} {{collapse bottom}} It can also be used as a stand-alone how-to page; a redirect to it, [[Help:User CSS for a monospaced coding font]], is categorized as such. It is also transcluded as a section, at [[Help:User style#User CSS for a monospaced coding font]]. {{Wikipedia technical help}} [[Category:Documentation templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how-to}}
{{navbar|Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|plain=y}}
 To consistently use a [[monospaced font]] with well-designed characters for coding so as to clearly distinguish between l, 1, and I, and between O and 0, and between -, −, –, and —, the system-default monospaced font can be changed: If like it as-is, you can simply @import (transclude) it: see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/codefont.css]] for quick instructions. Otherwise, copy one of the code snippets below to your [[Special:MyPage/common.css]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.css]] page, replacing {{kbd|"Roboto Mono"}} in the code with your preferred coding font. ([https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Roboto+Mono Roboto Mono] was used in this example as it is good, free coding font, for user-editable material on the wiki). This code will: * Apply a consistent monospace font of choice to all the normally monospaced HTML elements like {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|pre|o}}, etc. * Fallback to system-default {{samp|monospace}} font, should the chosen font be unavailable or lack the necessary characters. * Do the same for the output of all [[:Category:Example-formatting templates|Example-formatting templates]], such as {{tlx|mxt}} and {{tlx|xt}}. * Apply to additional site-wide classes identified so far (e.g., .monospaced) that output as monospace. * Make the three most frequently encountered editing fields also use this font stack: the main editing window, the edit summary line, and the search entry box. * If any additional [[:en:Class (computer programming)|classes]] are known that should be added, please update this page or mention it on [[Template talk:Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|the talk page]]. '''Horizontal style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button, .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} '''Vertical style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} {{anchor|Cleanup efforts}}'''Cleanup efforts''' If you'd like to help clean up instances of the {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}}} element – which has been discouraged since the 1990s, and should usually be replaced with {{tag|{{mxt|code}}}} (this may vary by context) – you can add something like the following to your {{samp|common.css}} to make {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}|o}} stick out like a sore thumb: {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Flag bad code for cleanup */ tt { color: DarkRed; background: Pink; } }} You can also do this with {{tag|font|o}}, {{tag|center|o}}, {{tag|strike|o}}, and other [[Wikipedia:HTML 5#Obsolete elements and attributes|deprecated elements]]. For CSS you can just import for this, see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/lint.css]].{{Documentation|content=This is a documentation snippet page transcluded (without the banner or this doc section) into other template documentation, and into [[Help:User style]], for consistency. It takes no parameters. Typical usage: == User CSS for a monospaced coding font == {{collapse top|left=y|title=Have monospaced templates in this group – and your editing window – use your preferred monospaced font:}} {{Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font}} {{collapse bottom}} It can also be used as a stand-alone how-to page; a redirect to it, [[Help:User CSS for a monospaced coding font]], is categorized as such. It is also transcluded as a section, at [[Help:User style#User CSS for a monospaced coding font]]. {{Wikipedia technical help}} [[Category:Documentation templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how-to}}
{{navbar|Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|plain=y}}
 To consistently use a [[:en:Monospaced font|Monospaced font]] with well-designed characters for coding so as to clearly distinguish between l, 1, and I, and between O and 0, and between -, −, –, and —, the system-default monospaced font can be changed: If like it as-is, you can simply @import (transclude) it: see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/codefont.css]] for quick instructions. Otherwise, copy one of the code snippets below to your [[Special:MyPage/common.css]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.css]] page, replacing {{kbd|"Roboto Mono"}} in the code with your preferred coding font. ([https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Roboto+Mono Roboto Mono] was used in this example as it is good, free coding font, for user-editable material on the wiki). This code will: * Apply a consistent monospace font of choice to all the normally monospaced HTML elements like {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|pre|o}}, etc. * Fallback to system-default {{samp|monospace}} font, should the chosen font be unavailable or lack the necessary characters. * Do the same for the output of all [[:Category:Example-formatting templates|Example-formatting templates]], such as {{tlx|mxt}} and {{tlx|xt}}. * Apply to additional site-wide classes identified so far (e.g., .monospaced) that output as monospace. * Make the three most frequently encountered editing fields also use this font stack: the main editing window, the edit summary line, and the search entry box. * If any additional [[:en:Class (computer programming)|classes]] are known that should be added, please update this page or mention it on [[Template talk:Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|the talk page]]. '''Horizontal style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button, .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} '''Vertical style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} {{anchor|Cleanup efforts}}'''Cleanup efforts''' If you'd like to help clean up instances of the {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}}} element – which has been discouraged since the 1990s, and should usually be replaced with {{tag|{{mxt|code}}}} (this may vary by context) – you can add something like the following to your {{samp|common.css}} to make {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}|o}} stick out like a sore thumb: {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Flag bad code for cleanup */ tt { color: DarkRed; background: Pink; } }} You can also do this with {{tag|font|o}}, {{tag|center|o}}, {{tag|strike|o}}, and other [[Wikipedia:HTML 5#Obsolete elements and attributes|deprecated elements]]. For CSS you can just import for this, see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/lint.css]].{{Documentation|content=This is a documentation snippet page transcluded (without the banner or this doc section) into other template documentation, and into [[Help:User style]], for consistency. It takes no parameters. Typical usage: == User CSS for a monospaced coding font == {{collapse top|left=y|title=Have monospaced templates in this group – and your editing window – use your preferred monospaced font:}} {{Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font}} {{collapse bottom}} It can also be used as a stand-alone how-to page; a redirect to it, [[Help:User CSS for a monospaced coding font]], is categorized as such. It is also transcluded as a section, at [[Help:User style#User CSS for a monospaced coding font]]. {{Wikipedia technical help}} [[Category:Documentation templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how-to}}
{{navbar|Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|plain=y}}
 To consistently use a [[:en:Monospaced font|Monospaced font]] with well-designed characters for coding so as to clearly distinguish between l, 1, and I, and between O and 0, and between -, −, –, and —, the system-default monospaced font can be changed: If like it as-is, you can simply @import (transclude) it: see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/codefont.css]] for quick instructions. Otherwise, copy one of the code snippets below to your [[Special:MyPage/common.css]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.css]] page, replacing {{kbd|"Roboto Mono"}} in the code with your preferred coding font. ([https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Roboto+Mono Roboto Mono] was used in this example as it is good, free coding font, for user-editable material on the wiki). This code will: * Apply a consistent monospace font of choice to all the normally monospaced HTML elements like {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|pre|o}}, etc. * Fallback to system-default {{samp|monospace}} font, should the chosen font be unavailable or lack the necessary characters. * Do the same for the output of all [[:Category:Example-formatting templates|Example-formatting templates]], such as {{tlx|mxt}} and {{tlx|xt}}. * Apply to additional site-wide classes identified so far (e.g., .monospaced) that output as monospace. * Make the three most frequently encountered editing fields also use this font stack: the main editing window, the edit summary line, and the search entry box. * If any additional [[:en:Class (computer programming)|classes]] are known that should be added, please update this page or mention it on [[Template talk:Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|the talk page]]. '''Horizontal style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button, .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} '''Vertical style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} {{anchor|Cleanup efforts}}'''Cleanup efforts''' If you'd like to help clean up instances of the {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}}} element – which has been discouraged since the 1990s, and should usually be replaced with {{tag|{{mxt|code}}}} (this may vary by context) – you can add something like the following to your {{samp|common.css}} to make {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}|o}} stick out like a sore thumb: {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Flag bad code for cleanup */ tt { color: DarkRed; background: Pink; } }} You can also do this with {{tag|font|o}}, {{tag|center|o}}, {{tag|strike|o}}, and other [[Wikipedia:HTML 5#Obsolete elements and attributes|deprecated elements]]. For CSS you can just import for this, see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/lint.css]].{{Documentation|content=This is a documentation snippet page transcluded (without the banner or this doc section) into other template documentation, and into [[Help:User style]], for consistency. It takes no parameters. Typical usage: == User CSS for a monospaced coding font == {{collapse top|left=y|title=Have monospaced templates in this group – and your editing window – use your preferred monospaced font:}} {{Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font}} {{collapse bottom}} It can also be used as a stand-alone how-to page; a redirect to it, [[Help:User CSS for a monospaced coding font]], is categorized as such. It is also transcluded as a section, at [[Help:User style#User CSS for a monospaced coding font]]. {{Wikipedia technical help}} [[Category:Documentation templates]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how-to}}
{{navbar|Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|plain=y}}
 To consistently use a [[:en:Monospaced font|Monospaced font]] with well-designed characters for coding so as to clearly distinguish between l, 1, and I, and between O and 0, and between -, −, –, and —, the system-default monospaced font can be changed: If like it as-is, you can simply @import (transclude) it: see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/codefont.css]] for quick instructions. Otherwise, copy one of the code snippets below to your [[Special:MyPage/common.css]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.css]] page, replacing {{kbd|"Roboto Mono"}} in the code with your preferred coding font. ([https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Roboto+Mono Roboto Mono] was used in this example as it is good, free coding font, for user-editable material on the wiki). This code will: * Apply a consistent monospace font of choice to all the normally monospaced HTML elements like {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|pre|o}}, etc. * Fallback to system-default {{samp|monospace}} font, should the chosen font be unavailable or lack the necessary characters. * Do the same for the output of all [[:Category:Example-formatting templates|Example-formatting templates]], such as {{tlx|mxt}} and {{tlx|xt}}. * Apply to additional site-wide classes identified so far (e.g., .monospaced) that output as monospace. * Make the three most frequently encountered editing fields also use this font stack: the main editing window, the edit summary line, and the search entry box. * If any additional [[:en:Class (computer programming)|classes]] are known that should be added, please update this page or mention it on [[Template talk:Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|the talk page]]. '''Horizontal style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button, .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} '''Vertical style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} {{anchor|Cleanup efforts}}'''Cleanup efforts''' If you'd like to help clean up instances of the {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}}} element – which has been discouraged since the 1990s, and should usually be replaced with {{tag|{{mxt|code}}}} (this may vary by context) – you can add something like the following to your {{samp|common.css}} to make {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}|o}} stick out like a sore thumb: {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Flag bad code for cleanup */ tt { color: DarkRed; background: Pink; } }} You can also do this with {{tag|font|o}}, {{tag|center|o}}, {{tag|strike|o}}, and other [[Wikipedia:HTML 5#Obsolete elements and attributes|deprecated elements]]. For CSS you can just import for this, see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/lint.css]].{{Documentation|content=This is a documentation snippet page transcluded (without the banner or this doc section) into other template documentation, and into [[Help:User style]], for consistency. It takes no parameters. Typical usage: == User CSS for a monospaced coding font == {{collapse top|left=y|title=Have monospaced templates in this group – and your editing window – use your preferred monospaced font:}} {{Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font}} {{collapse bottom}} It can also be used as a stand-alone how-to page; a redirect to it, [[Help:User CSS for a monospaced coding font]], is categorized as such. It is also transcluded as a section, at [[Help:User style#User CSS for a monospaced coding font]]. {{Wikipedia technical help}} [[Category:Documentation templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how-to}}
{{navbar|Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|plain=y}}
 To consistently use a [[:en:Monospaced font|Monospaced font]] with well-designed characters for coding so as to clearly distinguish between l, 1, and I, and between O and 0, and between -, −, –, and —, the system-default monospaced font can be changed: If like it as-is, you can simply @import (transclude) it: see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/codefont.css]] for quick instructions. Otherwise, copy one of the code snippets below to your [[Special:MyPage/common.css]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.css]] page, replacing {{kbd|"Roboto Mono"}} in the code with your preferred coding font. ([https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Roboto+Mono Roboto Mono] was used in this example as it is good, free coding font, for user-editable material on the wiki). This code will: * Apply a consistent monospace font of choice to all the normally monospaced HTML elements like {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|pre|o}}, etc. * Fallback to system-default {{samp|monospace}} font, should the chosen font be unavailable or lack the necessary characters. * Do the same for the output of all [[:Category:Example-formatting templates|Example-formatting templates]], such as {{tlx|mxt}} and {{tlx|xt}}. * Apply to additional site-wide classes identified so far (e.g., .monospaced) that output as monospace. * Make the three most frequently encountered editing fields also use this font stack: the main editing window, the edit summary line, and the search entry box. * If any additional [[:en:Class (computer programming)|classes]] are known that should be added, please update this page or mention it on [[Template talk:Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|the talk page]]. '''Horizontal style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button, .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} '''Vertical style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} {{anchor|Cleanup efforts}}'''Cleanup efforts''' If you'd like to help clean up instances of the {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}}} element – which has been discouraged since the 1990s, and should usually be replaced with {{tag|{{mxt|code}}}} (this may vary by context) – you can add something like the following to your {{samp|common.css}} to make {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}|o}} stick out like a sore thumb: {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Flag bad code for cleanup */ tt { color: DarkRed; background: Pink; } }} You can also do this with {{tag|font|o}}, {{tag|center|o}}, {{tag|strike|o}}, and other [[Wikipedia:HTML 5#Obsolete elements and attributes|deprecated elements]]. For CSS you can just import for this, see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/lint.css]].{{Documentation|content=This is a documentation snippet page transcluded (without the banner or this doc section) into other template documentation, and into [[Help:User style]], for consistency. It takes no parameters. Typical usage: == User CSS for a monospaced coding font == {{collapse top|left=y|title=Have monospaced templates in this group – and your editing window – use your preferred monospaced font:}} {{Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font}} {{collapse bottom}} It can also be used as a stand-alone how-to page; a redirect to it, [[Help:User CSS for a monospaced coding font]], is categorized as such. It is also transcluded as a section, at [[Help:User style#User CSS for a monospaced coding font]]. {{Wikipedia technical help}} [[Category:Documentation templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23515111839|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how-to}}
{{navbar|Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|plain=y}}
 To consistently use a [[:en:Monospaced font|Monospaced font]] with well-designed characters for coding so as to clearly distinguish between l, 1, and I, and between O and 0, and between -, −, –, and —, the system-default monospaced font can be changed: If like it as-is, you can simply @import (transclude) it: see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/codefont.css]] for quick instructions. Otherwise, copy one of the code snippets below to your [[Special:MyPage/common.css]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.css]] page, replacing {{kbd|"Roboto Mono"}} in the code with your preferred coding font. ([https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Roboto+Mono Roboto Mono] was used in this example as it is good, free coding font, for user-editable material on the wiki). This code will: * Apply a consistent monospace font of choice to all the normally monospaced HTML elements like {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|pre|o}}, etc. * Fallback to system-default {{samp|monospace}} font, should the chosen font be unavailable or lack the necessary characters. * Do the same for the output of all [[:Category:Example-formatting templates|Example-formatting templates]], such as {{tlx|mxt}} and {{tlx|xt}}. * Apply to additional site-wide classes identified so far (e.g., .monospaced) that output as monospace. * Make the three most frequently encountered editing fields also use this font stack: the main editing window, the edit summary line, and the search entry box. * If any additional [[:en:Class (computer programming)|classes]] are known that should be added, please update this page or mention it on [[Template talk:Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|the talk page]]. '''Horizontal style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button, .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} '''Vertical style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} {{anchor|Cleanup efforts}}'''Cleanup efforts''' If you'd like to help clean up instances of the {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}}} element – which has been discouraged since the 1990s, and should usually be replaced with {{tag|{{mxt|code}}}} (this may vary by context) – you can add something like the following to your {{samp|common.css}} to make {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}|o}} stick out like a sore thumb: {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Flag bad code for cleanup */ tt { color: DarkRed; background: Pink; } }} You can also do this with {{tag|font|o}}, {{tag|center|o}}, {{tag|strike|o}}, and other [[Wikipedia:HTML 5#Obsolete elements and attributes|deprecated elements]]. For CSS you can just import for this, see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/lint.css]].{{Documentation|content=This is a documentation snippet page transcluded (without the banner or this doc section) into other template documentation, and into [[Help:User style]], for consistency. It takes no parameters. Typical usage: == User CSS for a monospaced coding font == {{collapse top|left=y|title=Have monospaced templates in this group – and your editing window – use your preferred monospaced font:}} {{Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font}} {{collapse bottom}} It can also be used as a stand-alone how-to page; a redirect to it, [[Help:User CSS for a monospaced coding font]], is categorized as such. It is also transcluded as a section, at [[Help:User style#User CSS for a monospaced coding font]]. {{Wikipedia technical help}} [[Category:Documentation templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

{{Wikipedia how-to}}
{{navbar|Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|plain=y}}
 To consistently use a [[:en:Monospaced font|Monospaced font]] with well-designed characters for coding so as to clearly distinguish between l, 1, and I, and between O and 0, and between -, −, –, and —, the system-default monospaced font can be changed: If like it as-is, you can simply @import (transclude) it: see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/codefont.css]] for quick instructions. Otherwise, copy one of the code snippets below to your [[Special:MyPage/common.css]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.css]] page, replacing {{kbd|"Roboto Mono"}} in the code with your preferred coding font. ([https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Roboto+Mono Roboto Mono] was used in this example as it is good, free coding font, for user-editable material on the wiki). This code will: * Apply a consistent monospace font of choice to all the normally monospaced HTML elements like {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|pre|o}}, etc. * Fallback to system-default {{samp|monospace}} font, should the chosen font be unavailable or lack the necessary characters. * Do the same for the output of all [[:Category:Example-formatting templates|Example-formatting templates]], such as {{tlx|mxt}} and {{tlx|xt}}. * Apply to additional site-wide classes identified so far (e.g., .monospaced) that output as monospace. * Make the three most frequently encountered editing fields also use this font stack: the main editing window, the edit summary line, and the search entry box. * If any additional [[:en:Class (computer programming)|classes]] are known that should be added, please update this page or mention it on [[Template talk:Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|the talk page]]. '''Horizontal style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button, .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} '''Vertical style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} {{anchor|Cleanup efforts}}'''Cleanup efforts''' If you'd like to help clean up instances of the {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}}} element – which has been discouraged since the 1990s, and should usually be replaced with {{tag|{{mxt|code}}}} (this may vary by context) – you can add something like the following to your {{samp|common.css}} to make {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}|o}} stick out like a sore thumb: {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Flag bad code for cleanup */ tt { color: DarkRed; background: Pink; } }} You can also do this with {{tag|font|o}}, {{tag|center|o}}, {{tag|strike|o}}, and other [[Wikipedia:HTML 5#Obsolete elements and attributes|deprecated elements]]. For CSS you can just import for this, see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/lint.css]].{{Documentation|content=This is a documentation snippet page transcluded (without the banner or this doc section) into other template documentation, and into [[Help:User style]], for consistency. It takes no parameters. Typical usage: == User CSS for a monospaced coding font == {{collapse top|left=y|title=Have monospaced templates in this group – and your editing window – use your preferred monospaced font:}} {{Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font}} {{collapse bottom}} It can also be used as a stand-alone how-to page; a redirect to it, [[Help:User CSS for a monospaced coding font]], is categorized as such. It is also transcluded as a section, at [[Help:User style#User CSS for a monospaced coding font]]. {{Wikipedia technical help}} [[Category:Documentation templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24947213578|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how-to}}
{{navbar|Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|plain=y}}
 To consistently use a [[:en:Monospaced font|Monospaced font]] with well-designed characters for coding so as to clearly distinguish between l, 1, and I, and between O and 0, and between -, −, –, and —, the system-default monospaced font can be changed: If like it as-is, you can simply @import (transclude) it: see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/codefont.css]] for quick instructions. Otherwise, copy one of the code snippets below to your [[Special:MyPage/common.css]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.css]] page, replacing {{kbd|"Roboto Mono"}} in the code with your preferred coding font. ([https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Roboto+Mono Roboto Mono] was used in this example as it is good, free coding font, for user-editable material on the wiki). This code will: * Apply a consistent monospace font of choice to all the normally monospaced HTML elements like {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|pre|o}}, etc. * Fallback to system-default {{samp|monospace}} font, should the chosen font be unavailable or lack the necessary characters. * Do the same for the output of all [[:Category:Example-formatting templates|Example-formatting templates]], such as {{tlx|mxt}} and {{tlx|xt}}. * Apply to additional site-wide classes identified so far (e.g., .monospaced) that output as monospace. * Make the three most frequently encountered editing fields also use this font stack: the main editing window, the edit summary line, and the search entry box. * If any additional [[:en:Class (computer programming)|classes]] are known that should be added, please update this page or mention it on [[Template talk:Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font|the talk page]]. '''Horizontal style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button, .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} '''Vertical style''' {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Use my font, when available, for code */ code, pre, samp, kbd, tt, .example-mono, .userlinks-username, .monospaced, .keyboard-key, .button .plaincode { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } /* Make some of the editable stuff monospaced */ #wpTextbox1, #wpSummary, #searchInput, #searchText { font-family: "Roboto Mono", monospace !important; } }} {{anchor|Cleanup efforts}}'''Cleanup efforts''' If you'd like to help clean up instances of the {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}}} element – which has been discouraged since the 1990s, and should usually be replaced with {{tag|{{mxt|code}}}} (this may vary by context) – you can add something like the following to your {{samp|common.css}} to make {{tag|{{!mxt|tt}}|o}} stick out like a sore thumb: {{syntaxhighlight|lang=css|1= /* Flag bad code for cleanup */ tt { color: DarkRed; background: Pink; } }} You can also do this with {{tag|font|o}}, {{tag|center|o}}, {{tag|strike|o}}, and other [[Wikipedia:HTML 5#Obsolete elements and attributes|deprecated elements]]. For CSS you can just import for this, see [[meta:User:SMcCandlish/lint.css]].{{Documentation|content=This is a documentation snippet page transcluded (without the banner or this doc section) into other template documentation, and into [[Help:User style]], for consistency. It takes no parameters. Typical usage: == User CSS for a monospaced coding font == {{collapse top|left=y|title=Have monospaced templates in this group – and your editing window – use your preferred monospaced font:}} {{Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font}} {{collapse bottom}} It can also be used as a stand-alone how-to page; a redirect to it, [[Help:User CSS for a monospaced coding font]], is categorized as such. It is also transcluded as a section, at [[Help:User style#User CSS for a monospaced coding font]]. {{Wikipedia technical help}} [[Category:Documentation templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
